mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates above 5% signal deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite UNRATE > 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with stable inflation (CPI) indicates robust economic demand without price pressures, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns are negative when both GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL show positive trends.

mechanism_narrative: Weak industrial production during a supply shock (e.g., energy price spikes) leads to reduced corporate margins and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns despite INDPRO declining by > 2% in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: High residential sales (RSAFS) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) indicate strong housing demand and accommodative monetary policy, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns are negative when both RSAFS and FEDFUNDS show favorable trends.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) paired with low unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tight labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to elevated corporate earnings potential.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns despite PAYEMS rising > 100k and UNRATE falling < 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates during a monetary tightening cycle signal higher discount rates and reduced equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns despite FEDFUNDS rising > 0.5% in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust supply-side activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved corporate revenue visibility.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day returns are negative when both INDPRO and HOUST show positive trends.
